title: Telescope livestream: Watch the moon pass in front of the Pleiades on April 19
summary: A telescope livestream will allow viewers to watch the moon pass in front of the Pleiades star cluster on April 19. This celestial event can be observed from home.

title: Synthetic universe allows you to 'see and hear' galaxies evolving from the dawn of time (video)
summary: Scientists created a synthetic universe simulation that accurately models galaxy evolution from the dawn of time, fooling some astronomers into believing it's real.

title: Pragmata's tale of AI slop, humanity, & lunar conquest makes it the timeliest sci-fi game of the year
summary: Pragmata, Capcom's new sci-fi game, blends AI themes with lunar conquest, offering a refreshingly optimistic take. Its timely narrative and throwback action gameplay make it stand out as a notable release.

title: Artemis 2 and Tiangong space station astronauts set record for farthest distance between humans
summary: On April 6, the Artemis 2 astronauts and Tiangong space station crew set a record for the farthest distance between humans in space. This marked a historic moment in international space cooperation.
company: NASA and China Manned Space Agency

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 206 — I Want to Believe
summary: This Week In Space podcast Episode 206 features a discussion with Dr. Brianne Suldovsky about the implications of discovering alien life. The episode explores the societal and scientific impact of such a finding.

title: This life-hunting rover may be SpaceX's 1st-ever Mars launch
summary: SpaceX will launch Europe's Rosalind Franklin Mars rover in 2028, though not on its Starship rocket. The mission aims to search for signs of past or present life on Mars.

title: Blue Origin's reuses huge New Glenn rocket for 1st time, lands booster at sea (launch video)
summary: Blue Origin reused the first stage of its New Glenn rocket for the first time during its third launch, successfully landing the booster at sea. The mission took place on April 19 and was livestreamed.

title: How the 'Project Hail Mary' book walks the line between hard and speculative science fiction… and why the film did not
summary: The article discusses how Andy Weir's 'Project Hail Mary' balances hard and speculative science fiction, and compares the book to its film adaptation. It highlights the differences in storytelling between the two mediums.

title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
summary: The CSDA Quality Assessment Report has evaluated Satellogic's NewSat data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications. This adds to the growing documentation on the value of commercial satellite data.

title: Webinar 4/29: NASA CSDA Program Vendor Focus- MDA Space
summary: NASA's CSDA Program is hosting a vendor-focused webinar on April 29 featuring MDA Space. The event aims to provide insights into the program's vendors.

title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
summary: NASA's Human Research Program is developing methods to protect astronaut health for long-term missions on the Moon and Mars. The Artemis II mission is part of this effort, utilizing various research environments to monitor human health in deep space.

title: NASA, OPM Announce New NASA Force Website, Open Job Applications
summary: NASA and the U.S. Office of Personnel Management launched the NASA Force website to recruit top engineers and technologists for mission-critical roles in America's air and space programs. The initiative aims to support NASA's exploration, research, and advanced technology priorities.

title: Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Tomorrow.io Precipitation Radar Data
summary: A quality assessment report has evaluated the precipitation radar data from Tomorrow.io, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research. This adds to the growing evidence supporting the value of commercial data in scientific applications.

title: Volunteers Discover Rare Space Weather Events Using Their Ears
summary: Citizen scientists are helping NASA understand rare space weather events by analyzing data sonified as sound, comparing Earth's magnetic field to a 'harp in space.' The HARP project transforms magnetic field data into audible frequencies for easier pattern recognition.

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Observatory Maps Interstellar Ice in Milky Way
summary: NASA's SPHEREx Observatory has mapped the chemical signatures of water ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in the Milky Way's Cygnus X region. This discovery highlights the turbulent star-forming activity in one of the galaxy's most dynamic areas.

title: Spring Rains Saturate Michigan
summary: Michigan has experienced above-normal precipitation, leading to swollen rivers and damaged infrastructure. The spring rains have caused significant saturation across the state.

title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Latvia is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington, hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman. The event will include Latvia’s minister for education and science and the chargé d’affaires from the Latvian Embassy.

title: At the Edge of Light
summary: A photograph taken on April 6, 2026, captures the Moon’s far side near the terminator, highlighting long shadows and the Orientale Basin. The image showcases lunar surface details under low-angle sunlight.

title: New Glenn set to launch on third mission, reuse booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket is preparing for its third mission, marking the first reuse of its booster. The launch follows the rocket's second mission just five months prior.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an Astronaut Flight Suit and a Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality for future commercial space station operations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This milestone marks progress toward future Artemis missions following Orion's successful return.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and saw vehicle departures in March. The activities were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding its CRS NG-23 resupply flight.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marked a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after completing a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at its Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing preparations at the facility.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in a space industry news context, but no specific details about the news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 years of its hallmark achievements. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions over the past five decades.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes in-person and online sessions to gather feedback on space sector challenges. The event will take place on June 8-9, 2026, at ESA Headquarters in Paris, preceded by online preparatory sessions in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The signal was captured from an optimal location, demonstrating the system's capability.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in pointing technology for spacecraft, likely focusing on improved accuracy or efficiency. It highlights a specific innovation or development in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's 86th parabolic flight campaign. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials, offering potential for future satellite positioning and solar sail steering.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functionalities, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. This breakthrough could enhance satellite and spacecraft systems by consolidating various electronic components into a single chip.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is entering the thruster market with its new Gauss product line, aiming to vertically integrate and address scale concerns for customers. The move is part of Rocket Lab's broader strategy to expand its offerings beyond launch services.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is progressing with the development of its first qualification spacesuit, targeting a 2027 flight despite external uncertainties. The company's chief astronaut confirmed ongoing production efforts.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS) in 2026, breaking Axiom Space's initial monopoly on NASA's Private Astronaut Missions (PAMs). This marks a shift in the commercial space sector with new competitors entering the market.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulation, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, amid a surge in space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based communication technologies.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion and scientific potential of the space industry while questioning the financial models driving its execution.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's statements, while NASA invites them to disprove their claims. The dispute was discussed at the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions efficiently. The platform serves as a centralized solution for space operations.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has been launched as a new business unit focused on working with the Pentagon and NASA. The initiative is led by a former Texas representative.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted satellite companies advocating for in-flight connectivity solutions to airlines. Via Satellite reported key announcements related to this sector.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) to enhance customer relationships and improve the flying experience. The focus on IFC is highlighted as a key strategy in the aviation industry.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based systems for its operations.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the next phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing optical communication technology for space applications.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space security threats.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The Department of the Air Force awarded a contract for space-based air moving target indication (AMTI) development to multiple vendors. Meink stated that the risk associated with the project is low.

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform called Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites. This development aims to provide more advanced solutions in the satellite industry.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The software integrates Slingshot’s data flows with external sources to enhance analytics capabilities.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers as a viable alternative to terrestrial ones due to lower satellite launch costs and increasing demand. This shift aims to provide new architectural options for data storage and processing in space.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transition the communications layer of the SDA's Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture (PWSA) from SDA to a new acquisition executive. This change is part of the Space Force's broader acquisition structure rollout.
